33-35 Moroni made plans to attack the City Morianton. The Lamanites, notwithstanding their losses in other fields of warfare, were continually strengthening the City Morianton, which was on the east seashore. New supplies backed by added forces from other fields of conflict were making the city an exceedingly strong refuge into which the Lamanites might gather. Whether or not Moroni, despite his plans, attacked the city, we do not know. The Sacred Record makes no mention of it, and surmise is…

In these years He began to do long before He began to teach. (Farrar, p. 95.)

After a time, Alma departed for the land of Melek to teach the people there "according to the holy order of God." He was very successful and baptized many throughout the land. (Alma 8:4-5.) Alma next came to Ammonihah, where Satan's influence caused the people to harden their hearts against Alma's pure testimony. The prophet's labors provide a model for all missionaries who may have been called to work under difficult circumstances or who meet with little success: "Nevertheless Alma labored…

From the text of the passage referred to, some conclude that Alma traveled northward from Melek, but to us it conveys the idea that the prophet journeyed three days westward along or near the northern boundary of that land. From the text of the passage referred to, some conclude that Alma traveled northward from Melek, but to us it conveys the idea that the prophet journeyed three days westward along or near the northern boundary of that land. "And it came to pass" Most have heard Twain's quip that removing "and it came to pass" from the Book of Mormon would reduce it to a pamphlet. Who could blame him? Even in the present edition of the Book of Mormon "it came to pass" occurs 1297 times. However, this phrase also occurs 457 times in the KJV of the Old Testament. There, it's the English translation of the single Hebrew word, hâyâh. We tend to read this phase as indicating a passage of time. However, J Weingreen, in Practical Grammar…

He returned speedily] Although the idea of returning to the city of Ammonihah, to the abuse and scorn which he knew awaited him there, must have been an unpleasant one, Alma was obedient to the words and will of the Lord as delivered by the angel.
